title: Risk factor analysis and nomogram for predicting in-hospital mortality in ICU patients with sepsis and lung infection
country: USA (MIMIC-III database, Beth Israel Deaconess Medical Center)

sample_size.source_quote: After the inclusion and exclusion criteria were applied to the data set, 1676 patients with sepsis and lung infection were included in our study (Fig. 1).

icu_mortality_percent.source_quote: Survived to discharge N = 1191 Died in hospital N = 485

mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: SAPSII, heart rate, MBP, body temperature, SpO2, bicarbonate, BUN, creatinine, lactate, INR, renal failure, liver disease, cancer, organ transplantation, vasopressors, MV, CRRT, TnT, NLR, blood culture

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: Nomogram (age, lactate, temperature, oxygenation index, BUN, GCS, liver disease, cancer, organ transplantation, TnT, NLR, CRRT, MV, vasopressor use)
mortality_predictors.statistical_method: Logistic regression-based nomogram with AUROC comparison
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Multivariable model including age, lactate, temperature, oxygenation index, BUN, GCS, liver disease, cancer, organ transplantation, TnT, NLR, CRRT, MV, vasopressor use
mortality_predictors.source_quote: areas under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUROC) of 0.743 (95% C.I.: 0.713–0.773) and 0.746 (95% C.I.: 0.699–0.790) in the training and validation sets, respectively.
